# Travel Planning Rules

- Build itinerary with weather-first strategy for outdoor locations.
- Prioritize top-3 must-visit spots; avoid overpacking one-day plans.
- Add a fallback indoor option if precipitation probability is above 40%.
- Insert meal and transport breaks every 2 to 3 activities.
-

l and transport breaks every 2 to 3 activities.
- Use user preferences such as budget and interest tags (beach, museum, family) for personalization.

# Hangzhou One-day Travel Notes

- West Lake is always worth a visit, no matter what time or whether; parking can be crowded in noon.
- The tea museums have timed entry suggestions and usually lower crowd on weekday mornings.
- Hangzhou in March is always rainy; bring an umbrella.
- Consider groupin

lways rainy; bring an umbrella.
- Consider grouping attractions by area to reduce cross-city traffic time.
- Reserve at least 20 minutes transfer buffer between two major locations.

# Los Angeles One-day Travel Notes

- Griffith Observatory is best in early morning or sunset; parking can be crowded in noon.
- The Getty Center has timed entry suggestions and usually lower crowd on weekday mornings.
- Santa Monica is windy in late afternoon; bring a light jacket.
- Consider group

afternoon; bring a light jacket.
- Consider grouping attractions by area to reduce cross-city traffic time.
- Reserve at least 20 minutes transfer buffer between two major locations.
